summary: DHH describes how he used to skip unfamiliar words while reading online because looking them up was too disruptive. He solved this by adding a custom right-click menu option to his browser that opens a Merriam-Webster dictionary lookup in a small window. This small workflow improvement turned looking up words into second nature, improving his vocabulary and reading comprehension. He also created similar right-click tools for thesaurus lookups and Google searches.
key_points: Friction in workflows causes people to skip beneficial actions like looking up unfamiliar words
A small UX improvement (right-click dictionary lookup) transformed a cumbersome task into second nature
Browser customization through bookmarklets and context menus can meaningfully improve daily computing
Reducing the cost of an action changes behavior more effectively than willpower
money_quotes: Skipping words I didn't understand was how I read online for a long time.
The thinking went that getting the meaning of a sentence was good enough, as it was too cumbersome to look up something up and it would disrupt my reading if I did.
With this new right click-wonder looking up unfamiliar words is growing into second nature resulting in increased text understanding and vocabulary.
key_insight: Reducing friction in a workflow — even slightly — can transform a behavior you avoid into one that becomes second nature.
